meta.name: Shadow Research Missions
meta.description: Core PhD research missions for literature review, data analysis, academic writing, and dissertation progress. Each mission includes specific time estimates, measurable steps, and implementation notes.

tasks.title: Hunt 5 Relevant Articles for Literature Review
tasks.subject: Literature Review
tasks.successCriteria: 5 relevant, peer-reviewed sources saved with complete citations
tasks.failureCondition: Fewer than 3 usable sources or missing citation information
tasks.recoveryMission: Find 2 anchor papers only and save their citations

steps.action: Open your university library portal or Google Scholar
Write down 3 specific keyword combinations for your research question
Run each keyword search and save the top 10 results to a folder
Screen abstracts: keep only articles published in last 5 years from Q1/Q2 journals
steps.measurable: Abstract screening notes for each candidate

steps.action: Select the best 5 articles that directly address your research gap
Export citations in your reference manager (Zotero/Mendeley/Paperpile)

tasks.notes: Use your university's interlibrary loan if articles aren't freely available. Set a timer for each step to maintain momentum.

tasks.title: Extract Key Insights from One Article
tasks.subject: Literature Review
tasks.successCriteria: Article summarized in 5-8 sentences with 3 key quotes and 3 original insights
tasks.failureCondition: Article read without any captured notes

steps.action: Open the PDF and read the title, abstract, and conclusion first (5 min)
Identify the research question and theoretical framework (3 min)
steps.measurable: RQ and framework written in notes

steps.action: Scan the methodology section: note sample size, methods, key measures (5 min)
Highlight 3-5 key findings or arguments in the results/discussion (10 min)
Write 2-3 original insights connecting this article to your research (7 min)
Identify 1-2 limitations or unanswered questions (3 min)
Save annotated PDF and add citation to your literature matrix (2 min)

tasks.notes: Use a consistent color-coding system: yellow for findings, blue for methods, green for quotes you might use.

tasks.title: Update Literature Matrix for One Conceptual Theme
tasks.subject: Literature Review
tasks.successCriteria: Matrix updated with at least 5 new entries, columns filled completely
tasks.failureCondition: New articles added without filling in all matrix columns
tasks.recoveryMission: Add 2 new articles to matrix with minimal columns

steps.action: Open your literature matrix spreadsheet (Google Sheets/Excel)
Review existing column headers: Author/Year, Theory, Method, Sample, Key Findings, Gaps, Relevance
Add rows for 5 newly annotated articles from the past week
Fill in all 7 columns for each new article using your annotation notes
Identify patterns: which theories appear most? Which methods dominate? (8 min)
Write a 3-5 sentence synthesis of what this theme reveals about your topic (5 min)
Draft a gap statement: what's missing from this literature? (3 min)

tasks.notes: Keep your matrix as a master reference. Color-code rows by theme (e.g., blue for theory A, red for theory B).

tasks.title: Draft 500 Words for a Chapter Section
tasks.subject: Academic Writing
tasks.successCriteria: 500+ original words written, with at least 3 citations, forming a coherent argument
tasks.failureCondition: Editing existing text or writing fewer than 300 words
tasks.recoveryMission: Write 150 words from a bullet outline only

steps.action: Open your chapter outline and choose one specific subsection to draft
Gather all relevant annotated articles and matrix entries for this subsection
Write a clear topic sentence that states the paragraph's main claim
Draft 2-3 evidence paragraphs, each with: claim → evidence → interpretation (40 min)
Add in-text citations for each piece of evidence as you write
Write a synthesis sentence that connects this subsection to your main argument (5 min)
Write a transition sentence to the next subsection (5 min)
Save version with date in filename (e.g., Chapter1_v2_2026-04-18) (2 min)
Log word count and next action in your documentation (3 min)

tasks.notes: Don't edit while drafting. Just write. Editing comes later. Use the Pomodoro technique: 25 min write, 5 min break.

tasks.title: Integrate Advisor Feedback on One Section
tasks.subject: Revision & Submission
tasks.successCriteria: All feedback comments addressed, changes made, and response memo written
tasks.failureCondition: Reading comments without making any changes

steps.action: Open the draft and advisor's feedback document side by side
Read through all feedback once without making changes (just understanding)
Categorize feedback into: content, structure, clarity, citation (5 min)
Start with easiest fixes: typos, grammar, formatting (10 min)
Address medium-difficulty feedback: clarify unclear passages, add transitions (15 min)
Tackle hard feedback: restructure arguments, add new evidence (15 min)
Write a response memo: what you changed and why (3 min)
Save new version and highlight changes for easy review (2 min)

tasks.notes: Don't take feedback personally. Each comment is making your work stronger. If you disagree with feedback, note why and schedule a conversation.

tasks.title: Prepare Advisor Update Scroll
tasks.subject: Advisor Communication
tasks.successCriteria: Update document with wins, blockers, and 3 specific questions ready

steps.action: Open your advisor update template (Google Doc/Word)
List 3-5 concrete accomplishments since last meeting (3 min)
List 2-3 current blockers or stuck points (2 min)
Write 3 specific questions you need advisor input on (3 min)
Attach any relevant drafts, data, or figures (2 min)
Review for clarity and professionalism (2 min)
Save agenda and send to advisor 24 hours before meeting (2 min)

tasks.notes: Send your update 24 hours before the meeting. Advisors appreciate preparation and it makes meetings more productive.
